context_examples.translation: Mais j'ai peur que si ça continue comme ça, il y ait de moins en moins de choses avec des caractéristiques chinoises ?

global_config.visual_style: Cinematic, bright, modern, realistic style with a touch of travel vlog aesthetic. High-quality, 4K resolution.
global_config.setting_summary_cn: 两名美国学生张天明和丽莎获准到中国留学。他们先到上海，再乘高铁前往南京。天明的表哥在车站迎接他们，并开车带他们游览，让他们亲眼目睹了南京这座城市的巨大变化。
global_config.setting_summary_en: Two American students, Zhang Tianming and Lisa, get approval to study in China. They first arrive in Shanghai, then take a high-speed train to Nanjing. Tianming's cousin picks them up at the station and drives them around, showing them the great changes in the city.

character_definitions.name: 张天明
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A male Chinese-American student in his early 20s. He has short black hair, an expressive and curious face. Wears casual travel clothes like a hoodie, t-shirt, and jeans.

character_definitions.name: 丽莎
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A female Caucasian-American student in her early 20s. She has blonde hair, is observant and adventurous. Wears stylish but practical travel attire, like a light jacket over a sweater and a scarf.

character_definitions.name: 表哥
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A Chinese male in his late 20s, Tianming's cousin. He is friendly, confident, and proud of his city. Dressed in modern, smart-casual clothing. Drives a contemporary sedan.

hanzi: 张天明和丽莎上个学期末申请到中国学中文,原来以为时间会来不及,没想到学校很快就同意了。两个人可以在中国学习、生活三、四个月,这让他们非常高兴。
pinyin: zhāng tiān míng hé lì shā shàng ge xuéqīmò shēnqǐng dào zhōngguó xué zhōngwén yuánlái yǐwéi shíjiān huì láibují méi xiǎngdào xuéxiào hěn kuài jiù tóngyì le liǎng ge rén kěyǐ zài zhōngguó xuéxí shēnghuó sān sì ge yuè zhè ràng tāmen fēicháng gāoxìng
translation: At the end of last semester, Zhang Tianming and Lisa applied to study Chinese in China. They originally thought they wouldn't have enough time, but to their surprise, the school approved their application very quickly. They can study and live in China for three or four months, which makes them very happy.

scenes.scene_goal: 展示张天明和丽莎申请来华留学获批的喜悦过程。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 快速蒙太奇：张天明和丽莎在大学图书馆里，对着笔记本电脑填写申请，表情有些焦虑。镜头切换，丽莎在宿舍里刷新邮件，突然看到一封邮件，表情从紧张变为惊喜。她立刻视频通话告诉张天明，两人在屏幕两端兴奋地欢呼。
scenes.setting_en: A university library and a dorm room in the US.
scenes.camera_movement: Quick cuts, push-in on email notification.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic montage: A young Chinese-American man and a young Caucasian woman anxiously fill out an online application on a laptop in a university library. Cut to the woman in her dorm room, she refreshes her email, her face lights up with joy. She video calls the man, and they both celebrate ecstatically over the video call.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 张天明和丽莎上个学期末申请到中国学中文,原来以为时间会来不及,没想到学校很快就同意了。两个人可以在中国学习、生活三、四个月,这让他们非常高兴。
voice_over.pinyin: zhāng tiān míng hé lì shā shàng ge xuéqīmò shēnqǐng dào zhōngguó xué zhōngwén yuánlái yǐwéi shíjiān huì láibují méi xiǎngdào xuéxiào hěn kuài jiù tóngyì le liǎng ge rén kěyǐ zài zhōngguó xuéxí shēnghuó sān sì ge yuè zhè ràng tāmen fēicháng gāoxìng
voice_over.translation: At the end of last semester, Zhang Tianming and Lisa applied to study Chinese in China. They originally thought they wouldn't have enough time, but to their surprise, the school approved their application very quickly. They can study and live in China for three or four months, which makes them very happy.

hanzi: 张天明和丽莎先到上海,然后坐高铁去南京。车很快,一个多小时就到了。天明的表哥开车来接他们。表哥见到他们很高兴,说愿意当导游,带他们在街上走走,让他们好好儿看看南京。
pinyin: zhāng tiānmíng hé lìshā xiān dào shànghǎi rán hòu zuò gāo tiě qù nánjīng chē hěn kuài yí ge duō xiǎo shí jiù dào le tiānmíng de biǎo gē kāi chē lái jiē tā men biǎo gē jiàn dào tā men hěn gāo xìng shuō yuàn yì dāng dǎo yóu dài tā men zài jiē shang zǒu zou ràng tā men hǎo hāor kàn kan nánjīng
translation: Zhang Tianming and Lisa first went to Shanghai, and then took the high-speed train to Nanjing. The train was very fast, and they arrived in just over an hour. Tianming's cousin came to pick them up. The cousin was very happy to see them and said he would be their guide and show them around Nanjing.

scenes.scene_goal: 表现两人抵达中国，并与表哥见面的情景。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 快速转场：上海浦东机场的到达大厅，两人拖着行李箱兴奋地张望。接着是他们坐在现代化的中国高铁上，窗外景色飞速掠过。最后，在南京南站的出站口，一位阳光的年轻男子（表哥）向他们挥手，三人见面后热情拥抱。
scenes.setting_en: Shanghai Pudong Airport arrivals hall, inside a modern Chinese high-speed train, and the exit of Nanjing South Railway Station.
scenes.camera_movement: Panning shot following the characters, tracking shot from inside the train, steady shot of the reunion.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Travel vlog style montage: Zhang Tianming and Lisa excitedly pull their suitcases through Shanghai airport. Cut to them sitting on a sleek high-speed train, looking out the window as scenery blurs past. Finally, at the exit of Nanjing South Railway Station, a friendly Chinese man (Cousin) waves at them. They meet with a warm hug.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 张天明和丽莎先到上海,然后坐高铁去南京。车很快,一个多小时就到了。天明的表哥开车来接他们。表哥见到他们很高兴,说愿意当导游,带他们在街上走走,让他们好好儿看看南京。
voice_over.pinyin: zhāng tiānmíng hé lìshā xiān dào shànghǎi rán hòu zuò gāo tiě qù nánjīng chē hěn kuài yí ge duō xiǎo shí jiù dào le tiānmíng de biǎo gē kāi chē lái jiē tā men biǎo gē jiàn dào tā men hěn gāo xìng shuō yuàn yì dāng dǎo yóu dài tā men zài jiē shang zǒu zou ràng tā men hǎo hāor kàn kan nánjīng
voice_over.translation: Zhang Tianming and Lisa first went to Shanghai, and then took the high-speed train to Nanjing. The train was very fast, and they arrived in just over an hour. Tianming's cousin came to pick them up. The cousin was very happy to see them and said he would be their guide and show them around Nanjing.

hanzi: 爸爸常常说南京是个很安静的城市,没想到这么热闹!你看,大街上到处都是新盖的高楼,到处都是汽车。
pinyin: bàba chángcháng shuō Nánjīng shì ge hěn ānjìng de chéngshì méi xiǎngdào zhème rènao nǐ kàn dàjiē shàng dàochù dōu shì xīn gài de gāolóu dàochù dōu shì qìchē
translation: Dad often said Nanjing was a very quiet city, but I didn't expect it to be so bustling! Look, there are newly built tall buildings and cars everywhere on the main streets.

scenes.scene_goal: 通过张天明的视角，展现南京现代化、热闹的景象，表达他的惊讶。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 在表哥的车里，张天明坐在副驾驶座，他睁大眼睛，惊讶地看着窗外。他伸手指着窗外林立的摩天大楼和川流不息的车流，脸上是难以置信的表情。
scenes.setting_en: Inside a car driving through the bustling, modern streets of Nanjing, China.
scenes.camera_movement: Camera focuses on Zhang Tianming's face, then cuts to his point-of-view shot of the city.
scenes.video_prompt_en: View from inside a car driving on a modern Nanjing street. The camera focuses on Zhang Tianming in the passenger seat, his face filled with astonishment. He points out the window. POV shot showing towering new skyscrapers and endless streams of cars.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 爸爸常常说南京是个很安静的城市,没想到这么热闹!你看,大街上到处都是新盖的高楼,到处都是汽车。
voice_over.pinyin: bàba chángcháng shuō Nánjīng shì ge hěn ānjìng de chéngshì méi xiǎngdào zhème rènao nǐ kàn dàjiē shàng dàochù dōu shì xīn gài de gāolóu dàochù dōu shì qìchē
voice_over.translation: Dad often said Nanjing was a very quiet city, but I didn't expect it to be so bustling! Look, there are newly built tall buildings and cars everywhere on the main streets.

hanzi: 这些年南京的变化是很大,我以前骑自行车上班,现在也开起车来了。
pinyin: zhèxiē nián Nánjīng de biànhuà shì hěn dà wǒ yǐqián qí zìxíngchē shàngbān xiànzài yě kāi qǐ chē lai le
translation: Nanjing has changed a lot in recent years. I used to ride a bike to work, but now I've started driving a car too.

scenes.scene_goal: 通过表哥的亲身经历，侧面印证南京的巨大变化。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头转向正在开车的表哥，他微笑着回应张天明。画面中出现一个柔和的叠化效果：一个过去的表哥骑着自行车穿过一条相对安静街道的黑白画面，短暂地叠加在现在他开车的彩色画面上，然后消失。
scenes.setting_en: Inside a car driving through Nanjing.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Shot of the Cousin driving, he smiles and nods in agreement. A quick, stylized flashback overlay appears, showing him in black-and-white riding a bicycle on a quieter, older version of the same street, which then dissolves back to the present moment of him driving the car.

voice_over.speaker: 表哥
voice_over.hanzi: 这些年南京的变化是很大,我以前骑自行车上班,现在也开起车来了。
voice_over.pinyin: zhèxiē nián Nánjīng de biànhuà shì hěn dà wǒ yǐqián qí zìxíngchē shàngbān xiànzài yě kāi qǐ chē lai le
voice_over.translation: Nanjing has changed a lot in recent years. I used to ride a bike to work, but now I've started driving a car too.

hanzi: 这跟我以前想像的中国完全不一样。在上海的时候,要不是到处都是中文,我还以为是在美国呢。
pinyin: zhè gēn wǒ yǐqián xiǎngxiàng de Zhōngguó wánquán bù yíyàng zài Shànghǎi de shíhou yàobúshì dàochù dōu shì Zhōngwén wǒ hái yǐwéi shì zài Měiguó ne
translation: This is completely different from the China I had imagined. When we were in Shanghai, if it weren't for the Chinese characters everywhere, I would have thought I was in America.

scenes.scene_goal: 展现丽莎的视角，表达现代中国与她固有印象的巨大反差。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头给到后座的丽莎，她正看着窗外，表情混合着惊讶和思索。画面中出现一个想象气泡或柔焦效果，短暂地闪现一个她想象中的、充满传统建筑和灯笼的古老中国街道的画面，然后迅速切回她眼前现代化的南京街景。
scenes.setting_en: Inside a car, looking out at the modern city.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Camera focuses on Lisa in the back seat. She looks out the window with a thoughtful, bewildered expression. A brief, dreamlike visual effect shows a traditional Chinese painting of a village morphing into the modern Shanghai skyline with neon signs, then cuts back to her face.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 这跟我以前想像的中国完全不一样。在上海的时候,要不是到处都是中文,我还以为是在美国呢。
voice_over.pinyin: zhè gēn wǒ yǐqián xiǎngxiàng de Zhōngguó wánquán bù yíyàng zài Shànghǎi de shíhou yàobúshì dàochù dōu shì Zhōngwén wǒ hái yǐwéi shì zài Měiguó ne
voice_over.translation: This is completely different from the China I had imagined. When we were in Shanghai, if it weren't for the Chinese characters everywhere, I would have thought I was in America.

hanzi: 哎,天明,你看,那是什么地方?人那么多,旁边好像还卖吃的、玩儿的,五颜六色,真好看,真热闹!
pinyin: āi Tiānmíng nǐ kàn nà shì shénme dìfang rén nàme duō pángbiān hǎoxiàng hái mài chī de wánr de wǔ yán liù sè zhēn hǎokàn zhēn rènao
translation: Hey, Tianming, look! What's that place? There are so many people, and it looks like they're selling food and fun things nearby. It's so colorful, beautiful, and lively!

scenes.scene_goal: 展现丽莎初见夫子庙时的兴奋与好奇。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 三人走在街上，丽莎突然被远处热闹的夫子庙吸引。她兴奋地拉住天明的胳膊，指着前方，眼睛里闪着光。
scenes.setting_en: On a modern street in Nanjing, sunny afternoon. The traditional, colorful entrance to the Fuzimiao area is visible in the distance, bustling with people.
scenes.camera_movement: Starts as a medium shot of the three walking, then pans and zooms to follow Lisa's pointing finger towards Fuzimiao. Ends on a close-up of Lisa's excited face.
scenes.video_prompt_en: A young Caucasian woman with blonde hair (Lisa) stops abruptly on a sunny Nanjing street. She excitedly grabs her friend's arm and points towards a distant, bustling area with traditional Chinese architecture (Fuzimiao), her eyes wide with wonder. The scene is vibrant and full of life.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 哎,天明,你看,那是什么地方?人那么多,旁边好像还卖吃的、玩儿的,五颜六色,真好看,真热闹!
voice_over.pinyin: āi Tiānmíng nǐ kàn nà shì shénme dìfang rén nàme duō pángbiān hǎoxiàng hái mài chī de wánr de wǔ yán liù sè zhēn hǎokàn zhēn rènao
voice_over.translation: Hey, Tianming, look! What's that place? There are so many people, and it looks like they're selling food and fun things nearby. It's so colorful, beautiful, and lively!

translation: That is the Temple of Confucius.

scenes.scene_goal: 天明为丽莎解惑，点明地点。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头转向天明，他顺着丽莎指的方向看去，脸上露出温和的微笑，向她解释那就是夫子庙。
scenes.setting_en: On a modern street in Nanjing, with Fuzimiao in the background.
scenes.camera_movement: Cut to a medium close-up of Tianming.
scenes.video_prompt_en: A young Chinese-American man (Tianming) smiles warmly, looking towards the Fuzimiao temple complex off-screen. He speaks calmly, explaining what the place is. His expression is friendly and knowledgeable. Lisa is visible in the frame, listening intently.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.translation: That is the Temple of Confucius.

hanzi: 看起来,南京人真的是想尽可能保留老南京的特色、老南京的传统啊。
pinyin: kàn qǐ lái nán jīng rén zhēn de shì xiǎng jǐn kě néng bǎo liú lǎo nán jīng de tè sè lǎo nán jīng de chuán tǒng a
translation: It seems that Nanjingers really do want to preserve the character and traditions of old Nanjing as much as possible.

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎在观察中发出感慨，体会到南京对传统的保留。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 他们走近夫子庙，镜头扫过古色古香的建筑和人群。丽莎边走边看，脸上带着赞赏的表情，有感而发。
scenes.setting_en: Walking near the entrance of Fuzimiao, surrounded by traditional Chinese buildings and crowds of people.
scenes.camera_movement: Tracking shot following the characters as they walk. The camera focuses on Lisa's face as she observes her surroundings.
scenes.video_prompt_en: As the group walks closer to the Fuzimiao temple, the camera tracks them. The young Caucasian woman (Lisa) looks around in admiration at the traditional architecture and lively atmosphere, a thoughtful and appreciative expression on her face.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 看起来,南京人真的是想尽可能保留老南京的特色、老南京的传统啊。
voice_over.pinyin: kàn qǐ lái nán jīng rén zhēn de shì xiǎng jǐn kě néng bǎo liú lǎo nán jīng de tè sè lǎo nán jīng de chuán tǒng a
voice_over.translation: It seems that Nanjingers really do want to preserve the character and traditions of old Nanjing as much as possible.

hanzi: 南京一边是一栋栋高楼大厦,一边是一座座传统建筑,站在这儿,我好像听到了一种声音。
pinyin: nán jīng yī biān shì yī dòng dòng gāo lóu dà shà yī biān shì yī zuò zuò chuán tǒng jiàn zhù zhàn zài zhè'er wǒ hǎo xiàng tīng dào le yī zhǒng shēng yīn
translation: On one side, Nanjing has one tall building after another, and on the other side, one traditional building after another. Standing here, I seem to hear a sound.

scenes.scene_goal: 天明通过新旧对比，引出'历史的声音'这一感悟。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 天明停下脚步，站在一个能同时看到远处高楼和近处古建筑的街角。他神情变得若有所思，缓缓说出自己的感受。
scenes.setting_en: A street corner or bridge near Fuzimiao, offering a view that juxtaposes modern skyscrapers in the background with traditional architecture in the foreground.
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot focusing on Tianming. A slow pan from the skyscrapers to the traditional buildings, mimicking his gaze.
scenes.video_prompt_en: A young Chinese-American man (Tianming) stands pensively on a bridge. The camera shows his profile against a backdrop that contrasts modern skyscrapers with ancient-style buildings. He looks into the distance, a thoughtful expression on his face, as if listening to something only he can hear.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 南京一边是一栋栋高楼大厦,一边是一座座传统建筑,站在这儿,我好像听到了一种声音。
voice_over.pinyin: nán jīng yī biān shì yī dòng dòng gāo lóu dà shà yī biān shì yī zuò zuò chuán tǒng jiàn zhù zhàn zài zhè'er wǒ hǎo xiàng tīng dào le yī zhǒng shēng yīn
voice_over.translation: On one side, Nanjing has one tall building after another, and on the other side, one traditional building after another. Standing here, I seem to hear a sound.

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎对天明的感悟表示好奇，进行追问。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 丽莎听到天明的话，好奇地转过头看着他，直接地问他听到了什么。
scenes.setting_en: Same as previous scene, on the street corner/bridge.
scenes.camera_movement: Quick cut to a close-up of Lisa's face.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cut to a close-up of the young Caucasian woman's (Lisa) face. She looks at her friend (Tianming) with a curious and slightly puzzled expression, asking a question.

hanzi: 历史的脚步声。来中国以前爸爸让我找他上的那个中学。一会儿我给他发微信,告诉他南京变化太大,那个中学已经找不到了,再把今天拍的照片发给他看看。
pinyin: lìshǐ de jiǎobùshēng lái zhōngguó yǐqián bàba ràng wǒ zhǎo tā shàng de nàge zhōngxué yīhuìr wǒ gěi tā fā wēixìn gàosu tā nánjīng biànhuà tài dà nàge zhōngxué yǐjīng zhǎo bu dào le zài bǎ jīntiān pāi de zhàopiàn fā gěi tā kànkan
translation: The footsteps of history. Before coming to China, Dad asked me to find the middle school he went to. In a little while, I'll send him a WeChat message, tell him that Nanjing has changed too much and his school can't be found anymore, and then send him the photos I took today.

scenes.scene_goal: 天明解释'历史的脚步声'，并联系到自己家庭的记忆，深化主题。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 天明目光深远地回答，然后拿出手机，似乎在想念远方的父亲。他一边说，一边低头看着手机，像是在构思要发给父亲的信息。
scenes.setting_en: Same as previous scene, on the street corner/bridge.
scenes.camera_movement: Medium close-up on Tianming. The camera tilts down slightly as he pulls out his phone.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium close-up on the thoughtful Chinese-American man (Tianming). He explains with a sentimental tone, then pulls out his smartphone. He looks at the phone as he talks about sending a message to his father, his expression a mix of nostalgia and an awareness of change.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 历史的脚步声。来中国以前爸爸让我找他上的那个中学。一会儿我给他发微信,告诉他南京变化太大,那个中学已经找不到了,再把今天拍的照片发给他看看。
voice_over.pinyin: lìshǐ de jiǎobùshēng lái zhōngguó yǐqián bàba ràng wǒ zhǎo tā shàng de nàge zhōngxué yīhuìr wǒ gěi tā fā wēixìn gàosu tā nánjīng biànhuà tài dà nàge zhōngxué yǐjīng zhǎo bu dào le zài bǎ jīntiān pāi de zhàopiàn fā gěi tā kànkan
voice_over.translation: The footsteps of history. Before coming to China, Dad asked me to find the middle school he went to. In a little while, I'll send him a WeChat message, tell him that Nanjing has changed too much and his school can't be found anymore, and then send him the photos I took today.

hanzi: 哎,我也听到了一种声音。
pinyin: āi wǒ yě tīngdào le yī zhǒng shēngyīn
translation: Hey, I hear a sound, too.

scenes.scene_goal: 丽莎开玩笑，模仿天明，活跃气氛。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 丽莎听完天明的话，俏皮地眨了眨眼，模仿他刚才深沉的样子，说自己也听到了一种声音。
scenes.camera_movement: Focus on Lisa. She puts a hand to her ear playfully.
scenes.video_prompt_en: The young Caucasian woman (Lisa) breaks the serious mood with a playful smile. She mimics her friend's thoughtful pose, putting a hand to her ear as if listening intently to a secret sound.

voice_over.speaker: 丽莎
voice_over.hanzi: 哎,我也听到了一种声音。
voice_over.pinyin: āi wǒ yě tīngdào le yī zhǒng shēngyīn
voice_over.translation: Hey, I hear a sound, too.

scenes.scene_goal: 天明被丽莎的玩笑勾起好奇心。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 天明从思绪中回过神来，看到丽莎的样子，有些疑惑地问她。
scenes.camera_movement: Cut to a close-up of Tianming's face.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cut to a close-up of the Chinese-American man's (Tianming) face. He looks from his phone up to his friend (Lisa), his expression changing from pensive to curious and slightly confused by her joke.

hanzi: 肚子咕噜咕噜叫的声音。走了这么多路,你们好像不累不饿,可是我这个导游又渴又饿。别忘了,“民以食为天”啊!走,吃饭去!
pinyin: dù zi gū lū gū lū jiào de shēng yīn zǒu le zhè me duō lù nǐ men hǎo xiàng bù lèi bù è kě shì wǒ zhè ge dǎo yóu yòu kě yòu è bié wàng le mín yǐ shí wéi tiān a zǒu chī fàn qù
translation: The sound of a stomach rumbling. We've walked so much. You two don't seem tired or hungry, but I, your tour guide, am both thirsty and hungry. Don't forget, 'Ordinary people consider food as important as heaven'! Let's go, let's go eat!

scenes.scene_goal: 表哥用幽默的方式揭晓'答案'，将话题拉回到'吃'，并推动剧情发展。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 表哥突然插话，夸张地捂着自己的肚子。他一脸又累又饿的滑稽表情，然后大手一挥，号召大家去吃饭。丽莎和天明被他逗笑，三人一起朝美食区走去。
scenes.setting_en: Same as previous scene, then moving towards the food stalls of Fuzimiao.
scenes.camera_movement: Whip pan to the cousin as he interrupts. Medium shot of him being dramatic. Ends with a tracking shot from behind as the three laugh and walk towards the food stalls.
scenes.video_prompt_en: The Chinese cousin character dramatically interrupts, clutching his stomach with a comical expression of hunger. He breaks the serious mood, then grins and points enthusiastically towards the food stalls. The other two characters burst into laughter. The final shot shows the three of them walking away together into the bustling, food-filled market.

voice_over.speaker: 表哥
voice_over.hanzi: 肚子咕噜咕噜叫的声音。走了这么多路,你们好像不累不饿,可是我这个导游又渴又饿。别忘了,“民以食为天”啊!走,吃饭去!
voice_over.pinyin: dù zi gū lū gū lū jiào de shēng yīn zǒu le zhè me duō lù nǐ men hǎo xiàng bù lèi bù è kě shì wǒ zhè ge dǎo yóu yòu kě yòu è bié wàng le mín yǐ shí wéi tiān a zǒu chī fàn qù
voice_over.translation: The sound of a stomach rumbling. We've walked so much. You two don't seem tired or hungry, but I, your tour guide, am both thirsty and hungry. Don't forget, 'To the people, food is heaven'! Let's go, let's go eat!

global_config.teaching_style: A clear, step-by-step breakdown of the lesson dialogue, focusing on vocabulary and sentence structure for foundational understanding.
global_config.visual_style: Clean and modern, using cards and text animations to highlight key learning points.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand the context of the story: applying to study in China.

narration.subtitle_en: The story begins with Zhang Tianming and Lisa. This first part of the sentence tells us what they did: [zh:上个学期末申请到中国学中文]. Let's break it down. [zh:上个学期末] means 'at the end of last semester.' [zh:申请] is the verb 'to apply.' And their goal? [zh:到中国学中文] - 'to go to China to study Chinese.' So, they applied to study Chinese in China at the end of last semester.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 张天明和丽莎上个学期末申请到中国学中文,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng hé Lìshā shàng ge xuéqīmò shēnqǐng dào Zhōngguó xué Zhōngwén,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: At the end of last semester, Zhang Tianming and Lisa applied to study Chinese in China,

highlight_words.explanation_en: A compound word: 学期 (semester) + 末 (end).
A common verb used for applications to schools, jobs, or for visas.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a calendar icon flipping to the end of a semester, followed by an application form icon.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to express surprise and contrast between expectation and reality.

narration.subtitle_en: This part of the sentence expresses a pleasant surprise. It starts with [zh:原来以为], meaning 'originally thought.' They thought [zh:时间会来不及], that there wouldn't be enough time. The key phrase here is [zh:没想到], which means 'unexpectedly' or 'didn't expect.' What was the surprise? [zh:学校很快就同意了] - 'the school agreed very quickly.' This structure is great for telling stories about surprising outcomes.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 原来以为时间会来不及,没想到学校很快就同意了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yuánlái yǐwéi shíjiān huì láibují, méi xiǎngdào xuéxiào hěn kuài jiù tóngyì le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: They originally thought they wouldn't have enough time, but to their surprise, the school approved their application very quickly.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates that it's too late to do something or there isn't sufficient time.
Literally 'didn't think of,' used to introduce a surprising event or outcome.
Used when someone gives their consent or approval.

segments.visual_notes: Show a thought bubble with a clock and a worried face, then have it pop and be replaced by a stamped 'Approved' document.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to describe a duration of time and express happiness.

narration.subtitle_en: Finally, we learn about the result of the application. [zh:两个人] means 'the two of them.' They can [zh:学习、生活] (study and live) in China for [zh:三、四个月] (three or four months). Notice how consecutive numbers are used to express an approximation. The last clause, [zh:这让他们非常高兴], explains their feelings. [zh:这] refers to the whole situation, [zh:让] means 'to make' or 'to let,' and [zh:非常高兴] means 'very happy.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 两个人可以在中国学习、生活三、四个月,这让他们非常高兴。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: liǎng ge rén kěyǐ zài Zhōngguó xuéxí, shēnghuó sān, sì ge yuè, zhè ràng tāmen fēicháng gāoxìng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: They can study and live in China for three or four months, which makes them very happy.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Can be used as both a verb (to live) and a noun (life).
Using consecutive numbers like this is a common way to indicate an approximate range.
A causative verb that indicates someone or something causes a certain result or feeling.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for 'study' and 'live' next to a calendar showing 3-4 months. End with smiling character emojis.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to describe a sequence of travel using '先...然后...'.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, their journey begins. This sentence describes their travel plan using the structure [zh:先...然后...], which means 'first... then...'. First, [zh:先到上海], they arrive in Shanghai. Then, [zh:然后坐高铁去南京], they take the high-speed train to Nanjing. [zh:高铁] is the word for high-speed rail, a very common mode of transport in China.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 张天明和丽莎先到上海,然后坐高铁去南京。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng hé Lìshā xiān dào Shànghǎi, ránhòu zuò gāotiě qù Nánjīng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Zhang Tianming and Lisa first went to Shanghai, and then took the high-speed train to Nanjing.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common structure to describe a sequence of actions.
Short for 高速铁路 (gāosù tiělù). A key part of modern China's infrastructure.

segments.visual_notes: An animated map showing a path from Shanghai to Nanjing, with a high-speed train icon moving along it.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to describe travel time and speed.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence describes the train journey. [zh:车很快], 'the train was very fast.' How fast? [zh:一个多小时就到了]. [zh:一个多小时] means 'a little over an hour.' The word [zh:多] here indicates 'more than' or 'over.' The particle [zh:就] emphasizes that the arrival was quick or earlier than one might expect. It highlights the efficiency of the high-speed train.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 车很快,一个多小时就到了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Chē hěn kuài, yí ge duō xiǎoshí jiù dào le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The train was very fast, and they arrived in just over an hour.

highlight_words.explanation_en: When placed after a number and measure word, it indicates a quantity slightly more than that number.
Here, it implies that the action (arriving) happened quickly or sooner than expected.

segments.visual_notes: A clock face with hands spinning quickly for just over an hour, with a 'ding!' sound effect at the end.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn vocabulary related to meeting and greeting family.

narration.subtitle_en: Upon arrival, someone is there to meet them. [zh:天明的表哥], 'Tianming's older male cousin,' [zh:开车来接他们], 'drove to pick them up.' The verb [zh:接] means to pick someone up or to receive them. And [zh:表哥] is a specific term for a cousin, which we'll look at more closely.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 天明的表哥开车来接他们。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tiānmíng de biǎogē kāichē lái jiē tāmen.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Tianming's cousin came to pick them up.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A male cousin from the mother's side or father's sister's side who is older than you.
A verb-object compound: 开 (to drive) + 车 (car).
Used for meeting and collecting someone from a location like an airport or train station.

segments.visual_notes: An animation of a car pulling up to a train station platform where the characters are waiting.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand the specific kinship term '表哥'.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's focus on the word [zh:表哥]. Chinese kinship terms can be very specific. The character [zh:表] indicates a cousin with a different surname, typically from your mother's side or your father's sister's side. [zh:哥] means 'older brother.' So, a [zh:表哥] is your older, male cousin from your maternal family or paternal aunts. This is different from cousins who share your surname.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 表哥
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: biǎogē
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: older male cousin (with a different surname)
on_screen_text.notes: 表 (biǎo) denotes maternal cousins or paternal female cousins' children.

highlight_words.explanation_en: In kinship terms, it refers to relatives with a different surname.
Used for one's own older brother or as a suffix for older male cousins.

segments.visual_notes: A simple family tree diagram showing the relationship for '表哥'.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to offer to show someone around.

narration.subtitle_en: The cousin is very welcoming. He was [zh:很高兴] (very happy) to see them and said he was [zh:愿意当导游], 'willing to be their tour guide.' He offers to [zh:带他们在街上走走], 'take them for a walk on the streets,' and [zh:让他们好好儿看看南京], 'let them have a good look at Nanjing.' The reduplicated verb [zh:走走] and [zh:看看] suggests a casual, relaxed action, and [zh:好好儿] means to do something thoroughly or to one's heart's content.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 表哥见到他们很高兴,说愿意当导游,带他们在街上走走,让他们好好儿看看南京。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Biǎogē jiàndào tāmen hěn gāoxìng, shuō yuànyì dāng dǎoyóu, dài tāmen zài jiēshang zǒuzou, ràng tāmen hǎohāor kànkan Nánjīng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The cousin was very happy to see them and said he would be their guide and show them around Nanjing.

highlight_words.explanation_en: 导 (to guide) + 游 (to travel).
Refers to a public road in a city or town.

highlight_words.translation: to take a walk / to have a look
highlight_words.explanation_en: Reduplicating verbs like this softens the tone and suggests the action is brief and casual.

segments.visual_notes: An icon of a tour guide flag, followed by icons of walking and looking around a city map.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to express a contrast between a past impression and present reality.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, Tianming expresses his surprise. He starts with a past impression from his father: [zh:爸爸常常说南京是个很安静的城市], 'Dad often said Nanjing was a very quiet city.' Then, he contrasts it with the present reality using [zh:没想到], 'unexpectedly.' The reality is that it's [zh:这么热闹], 'so bustling!' The words [zh:安静] (quiet) and [zh:热闹] (bustling, lively) are opposites, highlighting the dramatic change.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 爸爸常常说南京是个很安静的城市,没想到这么热闹!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bàba chángcháng shuō Nánjīng shì ge hěn ānjìng de chéngshì, méi xiǎngdào zhème rènao!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Dad often said Nanjing was a very quiet city, but I didn't expect it to be so bustling!

highlight_words.explanation_en: Describes a low level of noise and activity.
Describes a place full of people, noise, and activity.

segments.visual_notes: A split screen: one side shows a serene, old-fashioned street (labeled '安静'), the other shows a modern, busy street (labeled '热闹').

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to use '到处都' to describe something being everywhere.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming continues describing the scene. He says [zh:你看], 'look,' to draw Lisa's attention. He uses the pattern [zh:到处都是] which means 'everywhere is...' or 'there are... everywhere.' What's everywhere? First, [zh:新盖的高楼], 'newly built tall buildings.' The verb [zh:盖] means 'to build.' Second, [zh:汽车], 'cars.' The repetition of [zh:到处都是] emphasizes the scale and density of the development.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你看,大街上到处都是新盖的高楼,到处都是汽车。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ kàn, dàjiē shang dàochù dōu shì xīn gài de gāolóu, dàochù dōu shì qìchē.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Look, there are newly built tall buildings and cars everywhere on the main streets.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to indicate that something is present in all places.
Specifically used for constructing buildings.
高 (tall) + 楼 (building).

segments.visual_notes: Animate skyscrapers and cars popping up all over a city street scene.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand how personal experience can illustrate broader societal changes.

narration.subtitle_en: The cousin confirms the changes with his own experience. He says [zh:这些年南京的变化是很大], 'Nanjing's changes in recent years have been huge.' He gives a concrete example: [zh:我以前骑自行车上班], 'I used to ride a bicycle to work.' [zh:现在也开起车来了], 'now I've also started driving a car.' This shift from [zh:自行车] (bicycle) to a car is a classic symbol of economic development and lifestyle change in China.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这些年南京的变化是很大,我以前骑自行车上班,现在也开起车来了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhèxiē nián Nánjīng de biànhuà shì hěn dà, wǒ yǐqián qí zìxíngchē shàngbān, xiànzài yě kāi qǐ chē lái le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Nanjing has changed a lot in recent years. I used to ride a bike to work, but now I've started driving a car too.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Can be a noun or a verb, referring to transformation.
Used for riding things you straddle, like a bike, horse, or motorcycle.
A verb-object phrase: 上 (to go to) + 班 (work shift).

segments.visual_notes: An animation showing a character on a bicycle, which then morphs into a car.

segments.teaching_goal: Distinguish between the verb '变' and the noun/verb '变化'.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's clarify the difference between [zh:变] and [zh:变化]. While both relate to change, their grammar is different. [zh:变] is a verb that can take an object, meaning 'to change into something.' For example, [zh:中学变成了一个购物中心], 'The middle school turned into a shopping center.' On the other hand, [zh:变化] is often used as a noun, as in our text: [zh:南京的变化很大], 'Nanjing's changes are big.' It can also be a verb, but it doesn't take an object, like [zh:天气变化得很快], 'The weather changed quickly.'

highlight_words.explanation_en: A transitive verb, often followed by 成 (chéng).
Can be a noun (a change) or an intransitive verb (to undergo change).

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to express that something is different from one's imagination.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa shares her impression. She says, [zh:这跟我以前想像的中国完全不一样]. This means, 'This is completely different from the China I had imagined before.' The structure is 'A [zh:跟] B [zh:不一样]' meaning 'A is not the same as B.' The key verb here is [zh:想像], 'to imagine.' The adverb [zh:完全] means 'completely,' emphasizing the degree of difference.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这跟我以前想像的中国完全不一样。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhè gēn wǒ yǐqián xiǎngxiàng de Zhōngguó wánquán bù yīyàng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: This is completely different from the China I had imagined.

highlight_words.explanation_en: 想 (to think) + 象 (image).
An adverb used to add emphasis.
The negative form of 一样 (yīyàng, same).

segments.visual_notes: Show a thought bubble with an old, traditional image of China, then have it shatter to reveal a modern cityscape.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn the hypothetical structure '要不是...' (if it were not for...).

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa elaborates on her feeling about Shanghai. She uses the structure [zh:要不是...我还以为...], which means 'If it weren't for... I would have thought...' She says, [zh:要不是到处都是中文], 'if it weren't for the Chinese characters everywhere,' [zh:我还以为是在美国呢], 'I would have thought I was in America.' This is a great way to make a strong comparison by stating a counterfactual condition.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 要不是到处都是中文,我还以为是在美国呢。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yàobúshì dàochù dōu shì Zhōngwén, wǒ hái yǐwéi shì zài Měiguó ne.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: If it weren't for the Chinese characters everywhere, I would have thought I was in America.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Introduces a hypothetical condition that is contrary to the facts.
Often implies that the initial thought was incorrect.

segments.visual_notes: Show a modern city scene. Animate Chinese characters appearing on buildings, then show an American flag icon with a cross over it.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to contrast the past and present regarding the presence of foreigners.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming adds another point of change, this time from his father's memory. [zh:他小时候], 'when he was little,' he [zh:很少看到外国人], 'rarely saw foreigners.' The word [zh:可是] introduces a contrast, meaning 'but.' [zh:可是现在], 'but now,' [zh:到处都有外国游客啊], 'there are foreign tourists everywhere!' The final particle [zh:啊] adds a tone of exclamation and emphasis, highlighting the surprise and scale of the change.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我爸爸说,他小时候在南京很少看到外国人,可是现在,你看,到处都有外国游客啊!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ bàba shuō, tā xiǎo shíhou zài Nánjīng hěn shǎo kàndào wàiguórén, kěshì xiànzài, nǐ kàn, dàochù dōu yǒu wàiguó yóukè a!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: My dad said that when he was little, he rarely saw foreigners in Nanjing, but now, as you can see, there are foreign tourists everywhere!

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'small time,' it refers to the period when one was a child.
外国 (foreign country) + 人 (person).
游 (to travel) + 客 (guest).

segments.visual_notes: A split screen. Left side: a black-and-white image of a street with only Chinese people. Right side: a color photo of the same street bustling with diverse tourists.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn the colloquial term '老外' and how to use it.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa makes a humorous point. She asks a rhetorical question: [zh:我们不也是两个“老外”吗]? 'Aren't we two 'foreigners' as well?' [zh:老外] is a very common and generally neutral colloquial term for 'foreigner.' It's less formal than [zh:外国人]. The structure '不也...吗?' is used to make a point emphatically, implying 'we are also...'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我们不也是两个“老外”吗?哈哈!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen bù yě shì liǎng ge “lǎowài” ma? Hāhā!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Aren't we two 'foreigners' as well? Haha!

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common, informal term for foreigners. 老 (old) + 外 (outside).

segments.visual_notes: An animation of the two main characters pointing at themselves with a speech bubble containing '老外'.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn vocabulary for different types of international businesses.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa points out more evidence of internationalization. She says, 'Look across the street,' [zh:你看马路对面]. She then lists examples of foreign businesses: [zh:美国快餐店] (American fast-food restaurant), [zh:日本银行] (Japanese bank), and [zh:法国服装店] (French clothing store). The ellipsis at the end suggests the list could go on and on.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你看马路对面,美国快餐店,日本银行,法国服装店……
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ kàn mǎlù duìmiàn, Měiguó kuàicāndiàn, Rìběn yínháng, Fǎguó fúzhuāngdiàn…
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Look across the street, an American fast-food restaurant, a Japanese bank, a French clothing store...

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to the other side of a street, river, or room.
快 (fast) + 餐 (meal) + 店 (shop).
银 (silver) + 行 (firm, business).
服装 (clothing) + 店 (shop).

segments.visual_notes: Animate storefronts with American, Japanese, and French flags appearing on a street.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to state that a phenomenon is widespread.

narration.subtitle_en: The cousin generalizes the observation. He says, [zh:中国其他地方也这样]. This means, 'Other places in China are also like this.' [zh:其他地方] means 'other places.' He concludes, [zh:变化真的很大], 'the changes are really big.' This confirms that the modernization they are seeing in Nanjing is a nationwide phenomenon.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 中国其他地方也这样,变化真的很大。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhōngguó qítā dìfang yě zhèyàng, biànhuà zhēn de hěn dà.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Other places in China are like this, too. The changes are really big.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to the rest of a group.
A general word for a location or area.

segments.visual_notes: Show a map of China with several major cities like Beijing and Shenzhen lighting up to show change is happening everywhere.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to express the concept of 'integrating into the world'.

narration.subtitle_en: Tianming offers a positive perspective on these changes. He says, [zh:中国融入世界了]. This means 'China has integrated into the world.' The verb [zh:融入] means to merge or integrate into. He then gives his opinion: [zh:这是件好事], 'This is a good thing.' The measure word [zh:件] is used for matters or things in an abstract sense.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 中国融入世界了,这是件好事。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhōngguó róngrù shìjiè le, zhè shì jiàn hǎoshì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: China has integrated into the world. This is a good thing.

highlight_words.translation: to integrate into, to merge into
highlight_words.explanation_en: 融 (to melt, to blend) + 入 (to enter).
A common noun for 'the world'.
好 (good) + 事 (matter, thing).

segments.visual_notes: An animation of a map of China connecting with other countries on a globe, followed by a thumbs-up icon.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to express worry and concern about a future trend.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa, however, expresses a concern. She starts with [zh:可是我担心], 'But I'm worried.' Her worry is about the future: [zh:这样下去], 'if this goes on.' The question she poses is, [zh:有中国特色的东西会不会越来越少呢]? 'Will there be fewer and fewer things with Chinese characteristics?' [zh:特色] means 'unique characteristics,' and [zh:越来越少] means 'less and less.' This introduces the central debate of the lesson: development versus tradition.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 可是我担心,这样下去,有中国特色的东西会不会越来越少呢?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Kěshì wǒ dānxīn, zhèyàng xiàqù, yǒu Zhōngguó tèsè de dōngxi huì bu huì yuèláiyuè shǎo ne?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: But I'm worried that if this goes on, will there be fewer and fewer things with Chinese characteristics?

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'to carry a burden on one's heart'.
特 (special) + 色 (color, feature).
Used before an adjective to indicate a progressive change.

segments.visual_notes: Show traditional Chinese items (like a lantern or calligraphy) slowly fading away, replaced by generic modern items. A question mark appears at the end.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand the use of '下去' to indicate continuation.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's look at the phrase [zh:这样下去]. The word [zh:下去] here is a directional complement, but its meaning is abstract. When placed after a verb or an adjective, it suggests the continuation of an action or a state. For example, 'to continue speaking' is [zh:说下去]. In our text, [zh:这样下去] means 'if it continues like this' or 'if this state persists.' It's a useful way to talk about the future consequences of a current trend.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 下去
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: xiàqù
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: to go on, to continue
on_screen_text.notes: As a complement, it indicates the continuation of an action or state.

highlight_words.translation: if it goes on like this
highlight_words.explanation_en: A set phrase to discuss the continuation of a current situation.
An example of V + 下去, indicating the continuation of the action '说' (to speak).

segments.visual_notes: An arrow pointing forward along a timeline, with the text '这样下去' above it.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express that a place has kept its traditional features despite modernization.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker starts with [zh:不会的], a common way to say 'No, that's not the case.' They then explain that Nanjing, [zh:南京], has still preserved, or [zh:保留了], many things with Chinese characteristics, [zh:有中国特色的东西]. The word [zh:特色] means 'characteristic' or 'distinguishing feature,' so [zh:中国特色] refers to things that are uniquely Chinese. This reassures the listener that not everything has become Westernized.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 不会的。南京还是保留了很多有中国特色的东西。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bú huì de. Nánjīng háishi bǎoliú le hěn duō yǒu Zhōngguó tèsè de dōngxi.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: No. Nanjing has still preserved many things with Chinese characteristics.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To keep something in its original state or from being damaged or lost.
A distinguishing quality or feature of something. '中国特色' means 'Chinese characteristics'.

segments.visual_notes: Show an image of a traditional Chinese gate next to a modern building to illustrate the concept.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to introduce an example using '比方说' and identify a key landmark.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, the speaker gives an example using [zh:比方说], which means 'for instance' or 'for example.' They point to architecture, [zh:建筑], as a key area where tradition is preserved. The sentence highlights that the place in Nanjing with the most Chinese characteristics, [zh:最有中国特色的], is the Temple of Confucius, or [zh:夫子庙]. This introduces the main destination for the characters.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 比方说建筑,南京最有中国特色的是夫子庙,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bǐfāng shuō jiànzhù, Nánjīng zuì yǒu Zhōngguó tèsè de shì Fūzǐmiào,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: For instance, architecture. The place in Nanjing with the most Chinese characteristics is the Temple of Confucius,

highlight_words.explanation_en: A phrase used to introduce an example, similar to 'for example'.
Refers to the design and construction of buildings.
A famous historical and cultural site in Nanjing.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 夫子 (fūzǐ) is a respectful term for a 'master' or 'teacher,' referring to Confucius. 庙 (miào) means 'temple'.

segments.visual_notes: An animated arrow points to the word '夫子庙' as it's mentioned, with a small picture of the temple appearing.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to list activities using the 'verb-的' pattern.

narration.subtitle_en: This part of the sentence describes what's available at the Temple of Confucius. The structure uses a pattern of verb + [zh:的] to turn actions into nouns. [zh:吃的] means 'things to eat,' [zh:喝的] means 'things to drink,' and [zh:玩儿的] means 'things to do for fun' or 'amusements.' The ellipsis at the end suggests the list goes on, emphasizing how lively the place is.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那儿还有吃的、喝的、玩儿的……
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: nàr hái yǒu chī de, hē de, wánr de...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: There are also things to eat, drink, and have fun with there...
on_screen_text.notes: The pattern 'Verb + 的' turns a verb into 'things to [verb]'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A nominalized phrase from the verb '吃' (to eat).
A nominalized phrase from the verb '喝' (to drink).
A nominalized phrase from the verb '玩儿' (to play/have fun).

segments.visual_notes: Use icons for food, drinks, and games to pop up as each phrase is mentioned.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to make a suggestion to go somewhere and try something.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a direct suggestion. It starts with [zh:那], meaning 'then' or 'in that case.' The speaker suggests, 'let's go to the Temple of Confucius now,' [zh:我们现在就去夫子庙]. The goal is to [zh:尝尝南京小吃], 'try some Nanjing snacks.' Notice the verb reduplication in [zh:尝尝], which softens the tone and implies trying something out casually. The particle [zh:吧] at the end also reinforces the suggestion.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那我们现在就去夫子庙,尝尝南京小吃吧。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nà wǒmen xiànzài jiù qù Fūzǐmiào, chángchang Nánjīng xiǎochī ba.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Then let's go to the Temple of Confucius now and try some Nanjing snacks.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Reduplication of the verb '尝' (to taste), suggesting a brief or casual action.
Literally 'small eats,' referring to a wide variety of local snacks and light meals sold by vendors or small restaurants.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 小 (xiǎo) means 'small' and 吃 (chī) means 'to eat'. Together, they form the concept of 'small eats' or snacks.

segments.visual_notes: An animation shows the characters walking towards an icon of the Fuzimiao, with a speech bubble containing images of various snacks.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the '不管...都/也...' structure for expressing 'no matter what'.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker agrees enthusiastically with [zh:好啊]. Then, they use the structure 'no matter what,' which is [zh:不管]. They say, 'no matter how China changes, no matter how Nanjing changes,' [zh:不管中国怎么变, 南京怎么变]. The one thing that hasn't changed, [zh:没变], is that 'people have to eat when they're hungry,' [zh:肚子饿了要吃]. This is a philosophical but humorous observation about a fundamental human need.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 好啊!不管中国怎么变,南京怎么变,肚子饿了要吃没变。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hǎo a! Bùguǎn Zhōngguó zěnme biàn, Nánjīng zěnme biàn, dùzi è le yào chī méi biàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Great! No matter how China changes or Nanjing changes, the fact that people have to eat when they're hungry hasn't changed.
on_screen_text.notes: 不管 (bùguǎn) means 'no matter' or 'regardless of'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to introduce a clause indicating that the result in the main clause will not be affected.
A verb indicating transformation or alteration.
Literally, 'the stomach is hungry'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a spinning globe labeled 'China' and a map of 'Nanjing' both transforming, but an icon of a person with a rumbling stomach remains constant.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask if someone has heard of a particular saying.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker then introduces a famous Chinese saying. They ask, 'Have you heard of...?' using the phrase [zh:你们听说过...吗]. The saying is [zh:民以食为天]. The word [zh:这句话] means 'this saying' or 'this sentence.' The final particle [zh:吧] softens the question, implying the speaker almost expects them to have heard it, though in this case, they haven't.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们听说过“民以食为天”这句话吧?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen tīngshuō guo “mín yǐ shí wéi tiān” zhè jù huà ba?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Have you heard the saying, 'To the people, food is heaven'?

highlight_words.explanation_en: '听说' means 'to hear,' and the particle '过' indicates past experience.

highlight_words.translation: To the people, food is heaven.
highlight_words.explanation_en: A classical Chinese idiom emphasizing the primary importance of food for ordinary people.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 民 (mín) means 'the people,' 食 (shí) means 'food,' and 天 (tiān) means 'heaven' or 'sky,' implying the highest importance.

segments.visual_notes: The idiom '民以食为天' is displayed prominently in stylized calligraphy.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express confusion and state that you've never heard something before.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a very natural and common response when you don't understand something. The speaker starts with [zh:啊], expressing surprise, followed by [zh:什么] for 'What?'. They then state clearly, [zh:从来没听说过], which means 'I've never heard of it.' The word [zh:从来] means 'from the past until the present' and is often used with a negative like [zh:没] or [zh:不] to mean 'never'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 啊?什么?从来没听说过。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Á? Shénme? Cónglái méi tīngshuō guo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Huh? What? I've never heard of it.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates that something has always been the case, or in negative sentences, has never been the case.

segments.visual_notes: An animation of a character with a question mark over their head.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to explain the meaning of a phrase simply.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker now explains the idiom. They begin with [zh:这句话的意思], 'the meaning of this saying.' To simplify, they use [zh:简单地说], which means 'simply put.' The core explanation is that [zh:吃], 'eating,' is the most important thing, [zh:最重要的事儿], in the lives of ordinary people, [zh:老百姓生活中]. This provides a clear and direct definition of the complex idiom.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这句话的意思,简单地说就是,“吃”是老百姓生活中最重要的事儿。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhè jù huà de yìsi, jiǎndān de shuō jiùshì, “chī” shì lǎobǎixìng shēnghuó zhōng zuì zhòngyào de shìr.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Simply put, it means that 'eating' is the most important thing in the lives of ordinary people.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A phrase used to introduce a simplified explanation.
A colloquial term for the general public or civilians.
The superlative form of '重要' (important).

segments.visual_notes: An animation shows a pyramid of needs, with a bowl of rice at the very top, labeled '吃'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to get someone's attention and ask about a place you see.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker exclaims [zh:哎] to get Tianming's attention, followed by [zh:你看], 'look!'. They ask, 'What's that place?', [zh:那是什么地方]. They then describe what they see: 'so many people,' [zh:人那么多]. This sentence is great for pointing something out and asking for information while describing the scene.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哎,天明,你看,那是什么地方?人那么多,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Āi, Tiānmíng, nǐ kàn, nà shì shénme dìfang? Rén nàme duō,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Hey, Tianming, look! What's that place? There are so many people,

highlight_words.explanation_en: An interjection used to call someone's attention or to express surprise or dissatisfaction.
Refers to a location or area.

segments.visual_notes: An animation of a character pointing excitedly towards a bustling scene off-screen.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn descriptive words for a bustling and colorful market scene.

narration.subtitle_en: Continuing the description, the speaker says [zh:旁边好像还卖吃的、玩儿的], 'it looks like they're selling food and fun things nearby.' They then use a colorful idiom, [zh:五颜六色], which means 'colorful' or literally 'five colors and six colors.' They end with two exclamations: [zh:真好看], 'so beautiful,' and [zh:真热闹], 'so lively!' The word [zh:热闹] is key for describing a bustling, exciting atmosphere.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 旁边好像还卖吃的、玩儿的,五颜六色,真好看,真热闹!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: pángbiān hǎoxiàng hái mài chī de, wánr de, wǔ yán liù sè, zhēn hǎokàn, zhēn rènao!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: it looks like they're selling food and fun things nearby. It's so colorful, beautiful, and lively!

highlight_words.explanation_en: An idiom meaning 'richly colored.' Literally 'five colors, six colors'.
Literally 'good to look at'.
Describes a place or a scene that is full of people, noise, and excitement.

segments.visual_notes: Show a vibrant market scene with colorful lanterns, food stalls, and crowds of people.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to confirm the identity of a place.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a simple, direct answer to the previous question. The speaker says, [zh:那就是夫子庙啊]. This means, 'That is the Temple of Confucius.' The phrase [zh:那就是] is used to confirm or identify something that was just pointed out. The final particle [zh:啊] adds a tone of explanation or emphasis, as if saying, 'That's the place we were just talking about!'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 那就是夫子庙啊。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nà jiù shì Fūzǐmiào a.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: That is the Temple of Confucius.

segments.visual_notes: The camera zooms in on the Temple of Confucius, confirming it's the place they were looking at.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express an observation about people's intentions to preserve culture.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker makes an observation starting with [zh:看起来], which means 'it seems' or 'it looks like.' They observe that the people of Nanjing, [zh:南京人], really want to, [zh:真的是想], preserve the character and traditions of old Nanjing as much as possible, [zh:尽可能保留老南京的特色、老南京的传统啊]. This sentence reflects on the cultural values visible in the city.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 看起来,南京人真的是想尽可能保留老南京的特色、老南京的传统啊。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Kàn qǐlai, Nánjīng rén zhēn de shì xiǎng jǐn kěnéng bǎoliú lǎo Nánjīng de tèsè, lǎo Nánjīng de chuántǒng a.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: It seems that Nanjingers really do want to preserve the character and traditions of old Nanjing as much as possible.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to introduce an opinion or conclusion based on observation.
To do something to the greatest possible extent.
Beliefs, customs, or ways of doing things that have existed for a long time.

segments.visual_notes: A split screen shows a modern part of Nanjing on one side and the traditional Fuzimiao area on the other.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the '一边...一边...' structure to describe two simultaneous states or contrasting scenes.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence paints a vivid picture of Nanjing's contrasts. It uses the structure [zh:一边...一边...], meaning 'on one side... on the other side...'. On one side, there are tall buildings, [zh:一栋栋高楼大厦]. On the other, traditional buildings, [zh:一座座传统建筑]. Note the use of reduplicated measure words, [zh:一栋栋] and [zh:一座座], to emphasize 'one after another' or 'many'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 南京一边是一栋栋高楼大厦,一边是一座座传统建筑,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nánjīng yìbiān shì yí dòng dòng gāolóu dàshà, yìbiān shì yí zuò zuò chuántǒng jiànzhù,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: On one side, Nanjing has one tall building after another, and on the other side, one traditional building after another.
on_screen_text.notes: The structure '一 + Reduplicated Measure Word' describes many similar objects.

highlight_words.translation: on one side... on the other side...
highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure used to describe two contrasting scenes or situations existing at the same time.
An idiom for high-rise buildings.

segments.visual_notes: An animation visually separates the screen, showing skyscrapers on the left and traditional temples on the right.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express a feeling or a metaphorical sensation.

narration.subtitle_en: Concluding the thought, the speaker says [zh:站在这儿], 'standing here.' They then describe a metaphorical feeling: [zh:我好像听到了一种声音], 'I seem to hear a sound.' The word [zh:好像] means 'to seem' or 'as if,' indicating this isn't a literal sound but a feeling or impression created by the contrasting scenery. This creates a sense of mystery and leads into the next question.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 站在这儿,我好像听到了一种声音。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhàn zài zhèr, wǒ hǎoxiàng tīngdào le yì zhǒng shēngyīn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Standing here, I seem to hear a sound.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates that something appears to be the case, but the speaker is not completely certain.
Refers to what can be heard.

segments.visual_notes: A character stands between the modern and traditional buildings, with a soundwave graphic emanating from the scene.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask a simple follow-up question for clarification.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a very short and direct question in response to the previous statement. The speaker simply asks, [zh:什么声音], 'What sound?'. This is a natural way to ask for more details when someone says something intriguing or unclear. It keeps the conversation flowing.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 什么声音?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shénme shēngyīn?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What sound?

segments.visual_notes: A large question mark appears on screen next to the character who is asking.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a character's reflection and the task given by his father.

narration.subtitle_en: The character starts with a poetic thought: [zh:历史的脚步声], 'The footsteps of history.' He's reflecting on the changes around him. Then, he recalls a task: [zh:来中国以前], before coming to China, his dad asked him to do something. The verb [zh:让] here means 'to ask' or 'to have someone do something'. What did his dad ask? To [zh:找], or 'find', the [zh:中学], middle school, that he attended.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 历史的脚步声。来中国以前爸爸让我找他上的那个中学。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lìshǐ de jiǎobùshēng. Lái Zhōngguó yǐqián bàba ràng wǒ zhǎo tā shàng de nàge zhōngxué.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The footsteps of history. Before coming to China, Dad asked me to find the middle school he went to.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to past events.
A compound word from '脚步' (footstep) and '声' (sound).
A versatile verb that can mean 'to let,' 'to allow,' or 'to ask someone to do something.'
Literally 'middle school', referring to junior and senior high school.

segments.visual_notes: Animate footsteps appearing on an old map, which then transitions to a modern city view.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe a plan and explain the reason for it.

narration.subtitle_en: The character outlines his plan. He says [zh:一会儿], meaning 'in a little while'. He will [zh:发微信], 'send a WeChat message'. The purpose is to [zh:告诉他], 'tell him', that [zh:南京变化太大], 'Nanjing has changed too much'. Because of this great change, [zh:那个中学已经找不到了], 'that middle school can't be found anymore'. The phrase [zh:找不到了] is a resultative complement indicating the action of 'finding' cannot be successfully completed.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 一会儿我给他发微信,告诉他南京变化太大,那个中学已经找不到了,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yīhuìr wǒ gěi tā fā Wēixìn, gàosu tā Nánjīng biànhuà tài dà, nàge zhōngxué yǐjīng zhǎo bu dào le,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: In a little while, I'll send him a WeChat message, tell him that Nanjing has changed too much and his school can't be found anymore,

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common time phrase indicating a short period of time.
'发' means 'to send' and '微信' is the most popular messaging app in China.
Can be used as both a noun ('change') and a verb ('to change').
A verb with a potential complement, indicating the inability to achieve the result of finding.

segments.visual_notes: Show a phone screen with a WeChat chat bubble being typed.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to use '再' and the '把' structure to describe a sequence of actions.

narration.subtitle_en: He continues his plan. The word [zh:再] here means 'then' or 'in addition', indicating the next step. He uses the [zh:把] structure, which brings the object forward to emphasize what is being done to it. What is the object? [zh:今天拍的照片], 'the photos taken today'. He will [zh:发给他], 'send them to him', so his dad can [zh:看看], 'have a look'. Reduplicating the verb '看' softens the tone and suggests a casual look.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 再把今天拍的照片发给他看看。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: zài bǎ jīntiān pāi de zhàopiàn fā gěi tā kànkan.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: and then send him the photos I took today.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates a subsequent action in a sequence.
A grammatical particle used to move the object of a verb before the verb itself.
A common word for photograph.
The reduplication of the verb '看' (to look) makes the suggestion more casual and gentle.

segments.visual_notes: Animate photos of modern Nanjing sliding into the phone screen from the previous scene.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to use '哎' and '也' to introduce a new, related idea.

narration.subtitle_en: Another character chimes in, starting with [zh:哎]. This is an interjection, like 'Hey' or 'Oh', used to get attention or express a sudden thought. She says, [zh:我也听到了一种声音]. The word [zh:也] means 'also' or 'too', connecting her experience to the previous mention of a 'sound'. She heard [zh:一种声音], 'a kind of sound', as well. This line builds suspense for the next question.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哎,我也听到了一种声音。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Āi, wǒ yě tīngdào le yìzhǒng shēngyīn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Hey, I hear a sound, too.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An interjection to call attention or express surprise or a sudden realization.
An adverb used to indicate similarity or inclusion.
A resultative verb compound: '听' (to listen) + '到' (to succeed in), meaning the action of hearing was successful.
A general term for any sound or voice.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a sound wave icon appearing next to the character's head.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask a simple follow-up question using '什么'.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a very common and simple question. [zh:什么声音] directly translates to 'What sound?'. The word [zh:什么] means 'what'. This is a natural and immediate response to the previous statement, asking for clarification. Notice how concise it is, which is typical in spoken Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 什么声音?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shénme shēngyīn?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What sound?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A fundamental question word used to ask about things.

segments.visual_notes: A large question mark appears on screen next to the text.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn an onomatopoeia for a stomach rumbling.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's the answer! The sound is from a [zh:肚子], which means 'stomach' or 'belly'. The phrase [zh:咕噜咕噜叫] is an onomatopoeia, a word that imitates a sound. It perfectly captures the 'rumble-rumble' sound a hungry stomach makes. So, the full phrase means 'the sound of a stomach rumbling'. It's a very vivid and humorous way to say you're hungry.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 肚子咕噜咕噜叫的声音。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dùzi gūlū gūlū jiào de shēngyīn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The sound of a stomach rumbling.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to the abdomen.
An onomatopoeia for the sound of a stomach rumbling or water gurgling.

highlight_words.translation: to cry, to make a sound
highlight_words.explanation_en: While it often means 'to be called', here it means 'to make a sound'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a cartoon stomach with sound waves coming out of it.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express contrast with '可是' and use '又...又...' to describe concurrent states.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker, who is the tour guide, explains why he's hungry. He starts with [zh:走了这么多路], 'We've walked so much.' He observes that the others, [zh:你们], 'you all', [zh:好像不累不饿], 'seem not tired and not hungry'. Then comes the contrast with [zh:可是], 'but'. He, the [zh:导游] or 'tour guide', is [zh:又渴又饿]. This 'both...and...' structure, [zh:又...又...], is used to link two adjectives describing the same subject. He is both thirsty, [zh:渴], and hungry, [zh:饿].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 走了这么多路,你们好像不累不饿,可是我这个导游又渴又饿。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zǒu le zhème duō lù, nǐmen hǎoxiàng bú lèi bú è, kěshì wǒ zhège dǎoyóu yòu kě yòu è.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: We've walked so much. You two don't seem tired or hungry, but I, your tour guide, am both thirsty and hungry.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to express an impression or likeness.
A common adjective for feeling fatigued.
Literally 'guide tour', a person who leads tours.
The '又...又...' pattern connects two adjectives, '渴' (thirsty) and '饿' (hungry).

segments.visual_notes: Split screen: one side shows two energetic tourists, the other shows a tired and hungry tour guide with thought bubbles for water and food.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn a famous Chinese idiom about the importance of food and how to make a suggestion.

narration.subtitle_en: The guide concludes with a powerful statement. He starts with [zh:别忘了], 'Don't forget'. Then comes the famous idiom: [zh:民以食为天]. This literally means 'the people take food as heaven,' emphasizing that for ordinary people, food is the most important thing. It's a cornerstone of Chinese culture. The particle [zh:啊] adds emphasis. Finally, he makes a clear suggestion: [zh:走,吃饭去], 'Let's go, let's go eat!' It's a direct and energetic call to action.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 别忘了,“民以食为天”啊!走,吃饭去!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bié wàng le, “mín yǐ shí wéi tiān” a! Zǒu, chīfàn qù!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Don't forget, 'Ordinary people consider food as important as heaven'! Let's go, let's go eat!
on_screen_text.notes: 民以食为天 is a famous saying from the Han dynasty.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common phrase used to remind someone of something.

highlight_words.translation: Food is the most important thing to the people.
highlight_words.explanation_en: A classical idiom meaning 'ordinary people consider food as important as heaven'. See Language Note LN-5.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 民 (people) 以 (take) 食 (food) 为 (as) 天 (heaven/sky).

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used here as an imperative to mean 'Let's go'.
A common, colloquial way to say 'let's go eat a meal'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the idiom '民以食为天' with a bowl of rice being lifted towards the sky. Then transition to a group walking towards a restaurant.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand and use the modal particle '啊' to express various emotions and soften the tone of a sentence.

narration.subtitle_en: Now let's focus on a very common particle in spoken Chinese: [zh:啊]. You'll hear it at the end of sentences all the time. Its main job is to add emotional color. First, it can heighten excitement or exclamation, like in the sentence [zh:到处都有外国游客啊], meaning 'there are foreign tourists everywhere!'. It can also be used in a declarative sentence to remind or explain something, as when the cousin says [zh:那就是夫子庙啊], 'That is the Temple of Confucius.' Another key function is to soften the tone of a question or a command, making it sound more gentle or casual. For example, you could ask [zh:我们什么时候走啊], 'When are we leaving?'. Notice that the sound of [zh:啊] can change depending on the final sound of the word before it, sometimes sounding like 'wa', 'na', or 'ya' for smoother pronunciation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: Q: 我们什么时候走啊? A: 好啊!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen shénme shíhou zǒu a? Hǎo a!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Q: When are we leaving? A: Great!
on_screen_text.notes: The particle 啊 (a) adds emotional color or softens the tone at the end of a sentence.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Used in exclamatory sentences to heighten emotion. Example: 到处都有外国游客啊! (There are foreign tourists everywhere!)
Used in declarative sentences to explain or remind. Example: 那就是夫子庙啊。 (That is the Temple of Confucius.)
Used at the end of a question to soften the tone. Example: 你怎么不说话呀? (How come you aren't saying anything?)
Used to tone down an imperative sentence. Example: 小心点儿啊! (Be careful!)

segments.visual_notes: Animate the '啊' particle appearing at the end of each example sentence with a subtle pop or glow effect to emphasize its role.

segments.segment_title: The Structure 以 X 为 Y
segments.teaching_goal: To learn the formal structure '以 X 为 Y' (yǐ X wéi Y), meaning 'to regard X as Y'.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, let's look at a more formal, literary structure: [zh:以 X 为 Y]. This pattern means 'to regard X as Y' or 'to treat X as Y'. It's constructed by taking an object X, and considering it as Y. A crucial pronunciation point is that the character [zh:为] here is a verb, pronounced in the second tone, [zh:为]. The most famous example, which you heard in the dialogue, is [zh:民以食为天]. This breaks down to '[zh:民]' (the people) '[zh:以]' (take) '[zh:食]' (food) '[zh:为]' (as) '[zh:天]' (heaven or the sky). So, it means 'The people regard food as the most important thing.' This structure adds a classic and formal touch to your Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 民以食为天。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Mín yǐ shí wéi tiān.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Ordinary people consider food as important as heaven.
on_screen_text.notes: The structure 以 X 为 Y (yǐ X wéi Y) means 'to regard or treat X as Y'.

grammar_points.explanation_en: A formal structure meaning 'to regard or treat X as Y'.
Example: Ordinary people consider food as important as heaven.

grammar_points.pattern: 我姐姐以帮助别人为自己最大的快乐。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒ jiějie yǐ bāngzhù biérén wéi zìjǐ zuìdà de kuàilè.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Example: My older sister regards helping others as her greatest joy.

segments.visual_notes: Visually break down the sentence '民以食为天' into its components (民, 以, 食, 为, 天) with their English meanings below each character.

segments.teaching_goal: To use the '一 + Reduplicated Measure Word' structure to describe a large number of similar items.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's examine a descriptive structure: 'one + a reduplicated measure word'. You saw this in the dialogue with [zh:一栋栋高楼大厦] and [zh:一座座传统建筑]. This pattern is used to describe a large group of similar, yet individual, objects. It creates a vivid image of 'one after another'. For example, [zh:一栋栋] paints a picture of many tall buildings, one next to the other. Similarly, [zh:一座座] suggests numerous traditional buildings. This is more descriptive than simply saying [zh:很多高楼] (many tall buildings). It emphasizes the quantity and the visual repetition of the items. Another example would be [zh:一盘盘水果], meaning 'plate after plate of fruit'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 南京一边是一栋栋高楼大厦, 一边是一座座传统建筑。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nánjīng yībiān shì yī dòng dòng gāolóu dàshà, yībiān shì yī zuò zuò chuántǒng jiànzhù.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: On one side, Nanjing has tall building after tall building; on the other, traditional building after traditional building.
on_screen_text.notes: The structure '一 + [Reduplicated MW]' describes many similar items, 'one after another'.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Describes a large grouping of identical or similar objects, implying 'one after another'.
Example: Tall building after tall building.
Example: Traditional building after traditional building.

grammar_points.pattern: 桌子上摆着一盘盘水果。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Zhuōzi shàng bǎizhe yī pán pán shuǐguǒ.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Example: On the table there is plate after plate of fruit.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of buildings or plates appearing one by one to visually represent the 'one after another' meaning of the reduplicated measure word.

segments.teaching_goal: To review the key vocabulary and grammar patterns from the lesson on 'Changes in China'.

narration.subtitle_en: Great job! In this lesson, we discussed the many changes in China. We learned how to express surprise with [zh:没想到], talk about past assumptions with [zh:原来以为], and describe things happening 'everywhere' with [zh:到处]. We also covered key grammar like the particle [zh:啊], the structure [zh:以...为...], and reduplicated measure words. Review these key phrases and try to use them to talk about changes in your own city.

highlight_words.translation: If it were not for... then...
Food is heaven for the people

segments.teaching_goal: Comprendre le contexte de l'histoire : postuler pour étudier en Chine.

narration_track.subtitle_en: L'histoire commence avec Zhang Tianming et Lisa. Cette première partie de la phrase nous dit ce qu'ils ont fait : [zh:上个学期末申请到中国学中文]. Décomposons cela. [zh:上个学期末] signifie « à la fin du semestre dernier ». [zh:申请] est le verbe « postuler ». Et leur objectif ? [zh:到中国学中文], c'est-à-dire « aller en Chine pour étudier le chinois ». Donc, ils ont postulé pour étudier le chinois en Chine à la fin du semestre dernier.

content.focus_text: 张天明和丽莎上个学期末申请到中国学中文,
content.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng hé Lìshā shàng ge xuéqīmò shēnqǐng dào Zhōngguó xué Zhōngwén,
content.focus_gloss_en: À la fin du semestre dernier, Zhang Tianming et Lisa ont postulé pour étudier le chinois en Chine,

highlight_words.explanation_en: Un mot composé : 学期 (semestre) + 末 (fin).
Un verbe courant utilisé pour les candidatures à des écoles, des emplois ou pour des visas.
Un mot composé : 学期 (semestre) + 末 (fin).

segments.teaching_goal: Apprendre à exprimer la surprise et le contraste entre l'attente et la réalité.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette partie de la phrase exprime une agréable surprise. Elle commence par [zh:原来以为], qui signifie « pensaient à l'origine ». Ils pensaient que [zh:时间会来不及], qu'il n'y aurait pas assez de temps. L'expression clé ici est [zh:没想到], qui signifie « de manière inattendue » ou « ils ne s'y attendaient pas ». Quelle a été la surprise ? [zh:学校很快就同意了], c'est-à-dire « l'école a accepté très rapidement ». Cette structure est excellente pour raconter des histoires avec des dénouements surprenants.

content.focus_text: 原来以为时间会来不及,没想到学校很快就同意了。
content.focus_pinyin: yuánlái yǐwéi shíjiān huì láibují, méi xiǎngdào xuéxiào hěn kuài jiù tóngyì le.
content.focus_gloss_en: Ils pensaient au départ qu'ils n'auraient pas assez de temps, mais à leur grande surprise, l'école a approuvé leur demande très rapidement.

highlight_words.translation: ne pas avoir assez de temps
highlight_words.explanation_en: Indique qu'il est trop tard pour faire quelque chose ou qu'il n'y a pas suffisamment de temps.
Littéralement 'n'y a pas pensé', cette expression introduit un événement ou un résultat surprenant.
Utilisé quand quelqu'un donne son consentement ou son approbation.
Indique qu'il est trop tard pour faire quelque chose ou qu'il n'y a pas assez de temps.
Littéralement 'n'y a pas pensé', utilisé pour introduire un événement ou un résultat surprenant.

segments.visual_notes: Afficher une bulle de pensée avec une horloge et un visage inquiet, puis la faire éclater et la remplacer par un document estampillé 'Approuvé'.

segments.teaching_goal: Apprenez à décrire une durée et à exprimer le bonheur.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Enfin, nous apprenons le résultat de leur candidature. [zh:两个人] signifie « les deux ». Ils peuvent [zh:学习、生活], c'est-à-dire étudier et vivre en Chine pendant [zh:三、四个月], soit trois ou quatre mois. Remarquez comment des nombres consécutifs sont utilisés pour exprimer une approximation. La dernière proposition, [zh:这让他们非常高兴], explique leurs sentiments. [zh:这] fait référence à toute la situation. [zh:让] signifie « faire » ou « laisser ». Et [zh:非常高兴] veut dire « très heureux ».

content.focus_text: 两个人可以在中国学习、生活三、四个月,这让他们非常高兴。
content.focus_pinyin: liǎng ge rén kěyǐ zài Zhōngguó xuéxí, shēnghuó sān, sì ge yuè, zhè ràng tāmen fēicháng gāoxìng.
content.focus_gloss_en: Ils peuvent étudier et vivre en Chine pendant trois ou quatre mois, ce qui les rend très heureux.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Peut être utilisé comme verbe (vivre) et comme nom (la vie).
L'utilisation de nombres consécutifs de cette manière est une façon courante d'indiquer une fourchette approximative.
Un verbe causatif qui indique que quelqu'un ou quelque chose provoque un certain résultat ou sentiment.

segments.teaching_goal: Apprenez à décrire une séquence de voyage en utilisant '先...然后...'.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Maintenant, leur voyage commence. Cette phrase décrit leur itinéraire avec la structure [zh:先...然后...], qui signifie « d'abord... ensuite... ». D'abord, [zh:先到上海], ils arrivent à Shanghai. Ensuite, [zh:然后坐高铁去南京], ils prennent le train à grande vitesse pour Nankin. [zh:高铁] est le mot pour le train à grande vitesse. C'est un moyen de transport très courant en Chine.

content.focus_text: 张天明和丽莎先到上海,然后坐高铁去南京。
content.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng hé Lìshā xiān dào Shànghǎi, ránhòu zuò gāotiě qù Nánjīng.
content.focus_gloss_en: Zhang Tianming et Lisa sont d'abord allés à Shanghai, puis ils ont pris le train à grande vitesse pour Nankin.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Une structure courante pour décrire une séquence d'actions.
Abréviation de 高速铁路 [zh:gāosù tiělù]. C'est un élément clé de l'infrastructure moderne de la Chine.
Une structure courante pour décrire une séquence d'actions.
Abréviation de 高速铁路 [zh:gāosù tiělù]. C'est un élément clé de l'infrastructure moderne de la Chine.

segments.visual_notes: Une carte animée montrant un trajet de Shanghai à Nankin, avec une icône de train à grande vitesse se déplaçant le long du parcours.

segments.teaching_goal: Apprenez à décrire le temps de trajet et la vitesse.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Cette phrase décrit le trajet en train. [zh:车很快], « le train était très rapide ». À quelle vitesse ? [zh:一个多小时就到了]. [zh:一个多小时] signifie « un peu plus d'une heure ». Le mot [zh:多] indique ici « plus de ». La particule [zh:就] souligne que l'arrivée a été rapide. Elle met en évidence l'efficacité du train à grande vitesse.

content.focus_text: 车很快,一个多小时就到了。
content.focus_pinyin: Chē hěn kuài, yí ge duō xiǎoshí jiù dào le.
content.focus_gloss_en: Le train était très rapide, et ils sont arrivés en un peu plus d'une heure.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Placé après un nombre et un classificateur, cela indique une quantité légèrement supérieure à ce nombre.
Ici, cela implique que l'action (l'arrivée) s'est produite rapidement ou plus tôt que prévu.
Placé après un nombre et un classificateur, cela indique une quantité légèrement supérieure à ce nombre.
